title: clematis viticella subsp. walteri
intro: Clematis viticella subsp. walteri, also known as Clematis walteriana or Clematis virginiana, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Clematis genus, known for its climbing habit and fragrant, star-shaped flowers. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in North America, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Clematis viticella subsp. walteri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. In herbal preparations, Clematis viticella subsp. walteri is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
